name: Hikma Health Inc
mission: We partner with nonprofit organizations providing healthcare in underserved and refugee populations. We create customized data systems for these healthc...
theoryOfChange: Hikma Health partners with nonprofit organizations providing healthcare in underserved and refugee populations to create customized data systems and offer clinicians data-driven tools. By making healthcare data accessible, secure, and actionable (including enabling patients to access/port their records and enabling clinicians to access authorized patient records across clinics), they aim to improve patient outcomes and support personalized, continuous care.

grantsData.purpose: Grant to Parkers Mobile Clinic, a non profit in Nigeria providing healthcare services to deploy the Hikma Health EHR system.

judge_issues.message: The impact rationale mentions a low cost per beneficiary as a positive aspect, but the score details indicate that the cost per beneficiary is implausibly low and results in a score of 0 for that component. This contradiction should be addressed.

The overall score rationale mentions 'exceptional financial efficiency' but the impact score is tempered by its role as a capacity builder. While accurate, it could be more specific about the negative impact of the implausibly low cost per beneficiary, which significantly reduced the impact score.
Evaluation source domain 'vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com' is not a known trusted evaluator
Website citation for 'beneficiaries_served_annually' uses homepage URL; deep link preferred

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization addresses the urgent cause of global health for underserved populations, making it a suitable choice for sadaqah-eligible giving [3]. While it offers a medium fit for Muslim donors, its focus on refugee populations aligns with common humanitarian priorities [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: Hikma Health demonstrates high directness through its Health and Environmental Record System (HERS) and Care Innovation Grants, achieving a low cost per beneficiary for its technical infrastructure [1][2]. Its 100.0% program expense ratio indicates that all spending is dedicated to these technological and health deployments [2].

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects a <cite id="14">verified theory of change</cite> and documented outcomes, though large-scale independent evaluations are not yet available.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: By providing EHR systems to clinics in underserved areas, Hikma Health enables better patient tracking and data-driven care, leading to improved long-term health outcomes.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that digital tools actually translate into better patient care in low-resource settings.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="12">declining revenue trend</cite>, the organization requires new capital to maintain its technical deployments.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an <cite id="2">offline-first technical architecture</cite> specifically for refugee settings.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="4">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that every dollar spent is dedicated to its core mission of health technology deployment.
Hikma Health demonstrates high stability with <cite id="8">26.0 months of working capital</cite> available to sustain operations.
The charity operates with maximum efficiency, spending <cite id="9">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions.

strengths_deep_dive: Hikma Health's technical depth is evidenced by its <cite id="2">offline-ready EHR system</cite>, which addresses the critical barrier of intermittent internet connectivity in refugee camps. Their commitment to transparency is reflected in their <cite id="10">Silver Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>. Furthermore, the organization has successfully managed <cite id="11">foreign grants totaling $8,000</cite>, demonstrating an ability to facilitate cross-border technical support and funding for local health initiatives.
rich_narrative.summary: Hikma Health Inc. is a specialized health technology nonprofit that empowers healthcare providers in underserved regions through <cite id="1">customized data systems and innovative health technology</cite>. The organization focuses on creating <cite id="2">offline-ready Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems</cite> designed specifically for refugee and vulnerable populations. In the 2023 fiscal year, Hikma Health reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $70,494</cite>, operating with a lean financial model where <cite id="4">1.0% of expenses were directed toward program services</cite>. Their work bridges the digital divide in global health by providing tools like the <cite id="5">Health and Environmental Record System (HERS)</cite> and specialized COVID-19 datasets to partners in regions such as <cite id="6">Nicaragua</cite>. By focusing on capacity building rather than direct clinical care, the organization enables local clinics to <cite id="7">improve data collection and patient outcomes</cite> in resource-constrained environments.
